task:
Create Digital Fortress middleware and tests:
1) Create file src/middleware/fortress.js that exports:
   - an Express middleware function that sets these response headers: X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer. When environment variable FORTRESS_HSTS=true, also set Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload.
   - a Koa middleware (async (ctx, next)) that sets the same headers (and HSTS when FORTRESS_HSTS=true).
   - an Express Router exported as router that defines GET /status/fo returning 200 JSON { status: "ok", fortress: true }.
   Use CommonJS or ESM based on existing project conventions if detectable; otherwise use CommonJS.
2) Edit src/server.js to import/require the fortress module and:
   - apply the Express middleware early (app.use(fortress.middleware) or app.use(fortress)) before other routes
   - mount the router at root (app.use(fortress.router) or app.use('/', fortress.router))
   Ensure edits are minimally invasive and preserve existing functionality.
3) Add test/test_fortress.test.js using Jest + SuperTest that:
   - starts the app (require('../src/server') or the exported app) and uses supertest to GET /status/fo
   - asserts status 200 and body { status: 'ok', fortress: true }
   - asserts response contains headers: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy; and when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true' set, Strict-Transport-Security is present.
4) Run tests (npm test or npx jest) and report results.
Place changes under project path: C:\Users\user\Desktop\Joi AI. Mode: sequential.

subtasks.description: Create a new middleware module that exports: (a) an Express middleware function that sets the security headers (and HSTS if FORTRESS_HSTS=true), (b) a Koa middleware (async (ctx, next)) that sets the same headers, and (c) an Express Router with GET /status/fo -> 200 JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }. Use CommonJS exports.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: File src/middleware/fortress.js exists and when required exports an object with properties: middleware (Express-compatible function (req,res,next) or the middleware function itself), koa (async function (ctx,next)), and router (an Express Router). The middleware/koa must set headers: X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block, Referrer-Policy: no-referrer. If process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS === 'true', also set Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. The router must expose GET /status/fo returning JSON { status: 'ok', fortress: true }.

changes.explanation: Create new middleware module exporting Express middleware, Koa middleware, and Express Router. Sets required security headers and optional HSTS based on FORTRESS_HSTS.

subtasks.description: Edit src/server.js to require/import the fortress module and apply the Express middleware early (before other routes) and mount the exported router at root. Ensure the server file still exports the Express app (module.exports = app) so tests can require the app. Keep changes minimal and non-disruptive.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: src/server.js is updated to require the fortress module (CommonJS require('./middleware/fortress') or equivalent), calls app.use(fortress.middleware) or app.use(fortress) as early as possible (before other routes), and mounts the router with app.use('/', fortress.router). The file exports the app (module.exports = app) if it did not already. Other server behavior remains intact.

subtasks.description: Create Jest + SuperTest test file that starts/uses the app and asserts the /status/fo endpoint returns 200 and the expected JSON and headers; include a test verifying Strict-Transport-Security header appears when process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true'.
subtasks.acceptance_criteria: test/test_fortress.test.js exists and running the test command passes. The test suite must: (a) require the app from src/server.js (ensuring app is exported), (b) perform a GET /status/fo and assert status 200 and body equals { status: 'ok', fortress: true }, (c) assert that response headers include X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, and (d) set process.env.FORTRESS_HSTS='true' for one test run (or within a separate test) to assert Strict-Transport-Security header is present.

plan_summary: Add a Digital Fortress middleware that provides Express and Koa middleware plus an Express router; wire it into the existing server early; add Jest+SuperTest tests that verify headers and endpoint; run tests.
risk_assessment: Medium - Potential issues: inability to detect project's exact module style (we chose CommonJS), requiring src/server.js during tests may start a live listener if server.js calls app.listen unconditionally (we ask coder to export app without changing behavior otherwise), and dependency/version mismatches for Jest/SuperTest could cause test failures.
